name: Rhea
shortDescription: Rhea is a Titan goddess in Greek mythology, the daughter of Gaia and Uranus, and the wife of Cronus. She is primarily known as the mother of the Olympian gods.
description: Rhea is a Titan goddess in Greek mythology, daughter of Gaia (Earth) and Uranus (Sky). She is primarily known as the mother of the Olympian gods and is often referred to as the "Mother of the Gods." Her husband was her brother, Cronus, who, fearing a prophecy that he would be overthrown by his own children, swallowed each of them at birth. To save her youngest child, Zeus, Rhea tricked Cronus by wrapping a stone in swaddling clothes and giving it to him to swallow instead. She hid Zeus in a cave on Mount Ida in Crete, where he was raised in secret. Rhea's act of defiance against Cronus ensured the survival of Zeus and the eventual overthrow of the Titans by the Olympian gods, marking a pivotal moment in Greek mythology. As a Titan goddess, Rhea is associated with fertility, motherhood, and generation. She is sometimes depicted with lions, symbolizing her power and dominion. Her role as the mother of the Olympian gods underscores her importance in the Greek pantheon, connecting her to the establishment of the new order of gods and goddesses. Her association with Demeter through their shared domains of fertility and motherhood and the Eleusinian Mysteries highlights her connection to the cycles of life and death, and the renewal of the earth.
